title: Number Representation & Place Value
objective: Students will explore number representation & place value concepts including Place Value × 10 Pattern, Reading and writing numbers (age 9+), Negative numbers in context and more.

steps.content: Welcome to an exciting lesson about Place Value × 10 Pattern!
Recognise that in a multi-digit whole number, a digit in one place represents ten times what it represents in the place to its right (e.g. 700 ÷ 70 = 10)
Let me show you something cool. Explain that the 3 in 3,000 is ten times the 3 in 300
Can you explain that in 700, the 7 is worth 10 times more than in 70 — because moving one place to the left multiplies the value by 10?
Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Place Value × 10 Pattern. Explain that the 3 in 3,000 is ten times the 3 in 300 Complete: 700 ÷ 70 = __ and explain using place-value reasoning
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Reading and writing numbers (age 9+)!
Read, write, order, and compare whole numbers up to at least 1,000,000 using base-ten numerals, number names, expanded form, and place-value understanding
Let me show you something cool. Write 403,072 in words and in expanded form
Can you read and write numbers into the millions — like 1,340,000 — and put a set of large numbers in order correctly?
You did it! Here's what we explored about Reading and writing numbers (age 9+): Write 403,072 in words and in expanded form Compare 548,301 and 543,801 using < and explain the reasoning
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Negative numbers in context!
Interpret negative numbers in context (temperature, sea level, bank balance); count forwards and backwards with positive and negative whole numbers, including through zero
Time to try this out: Place –3, –1, 0, 2, 5 on a number line
If the temperature drops to −5°C overnight and rises to 3°C by day, can you place both on a number line and say how many degrees warmer the daytime temperature is?
Great job! Today we learned about Negative numbers in context. Place –3, –1, 0, 2, 5 on a number line The temperature is –4°C and rises by 7 degrees — what is the new temperature?
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Rounding Large Numbers!
Round any whole number up to 1,000,000 to the nearest 10, 100, 1,000, 10,000, or 100,000 using place-value understanding
Let's practice together! Round 456,782 to the nearest 10,000 (460,000)
Can you round a large number like 346,712 to the nearest 1,000 (347,000) or 10,000 (350,000) — identifying which digit to look at for each rounding?
Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Rounding Large Numbers. Round 456,782 to the nearest 10,000 (460,000) Round 950,500 to the nearest 100,000 and explain the boundary case
Let's explore Working with Large Numbers! Have you ever wondered about this?
Solve number and practical problems involving reading, writing, ordering, comparing, and rounding whole numbers up to 1,000,000
Let me show you something cool. What is the largest six-digit number with digits summing to 15?
If you is told the population of a city is 2,347,819, can they read that number aloud correctly and say roughly how many millions it is?

Great job! Today we learned about Working with Large Numbers. What is the largest six-digit number with digits summing to 15? A stadium holds 67,450 people. Round to the nearest thousand for a news report
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Counting forwards and backwards (age 9+)!
Count forwards and backwards in steps of powers of 10 (10, 100, 1000, 10,000, 100,000) for any given number up to 1,000,000
Let's practice together! Count on in 10,000s from 462,000
Can you count forwards and backwards in steps of 1,000 or 10,000 from any starting number — like 34,500, 44,500, 54,500 — without losing track?
Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Counting forwards and backwards (age 9+). Count on in 10,000s from 462,000 Count back in 100,000s from 800,000
Today we're going to learn about Roman numerals to 1000. Get ready for an adventure!
Read Roman numerals to 1000 (M) and recognise years written in Roman numerals
Let me show you something cool. Read MCMXCIX as 1999
Can you read Roman numerals like MMXIX (2019) or CDXLIV (444) — and recognise the year a film or building was made when it's shown in Roman numerals?
Great job! Today we learned about Roman numerals to 1000. Read MCMXCIX as 1999 Write 2024 in Roman numerals (MMXXIV)
